QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_73

question.text: STT is NOT applicable on transactions in which of the following?

answer.text: Currency derivatives and interest rate derivatives

explanation.text: **The Logic:** STT is NOT applicable on Currency Derivatives, Interest Rate Derivatives, and Debt Securities transactions. STT applies only to equity segment (cash and derivatives) transactions. **The Trap:** Even physically-settled stock derivatives attract STT at delivery rates. But currency and IR derivatives are fully exempt regardless of settlement method. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series I and IV cover the STT-exempt status of currency and IR derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** STT exemption makes currency and IR derivatives cost-effective for hedging and trading. This is one reason currency derivative volumes have grown — the zero STT environment reduces transaction costs significantly.
